note: The about:gjoa settings registry. gjoa's single settings home. Each setting is a live pref (present + reversible). Privacy profiles are CURATED presets — pick one and it flips a curated subset of the granular toggles; hand-editing any toggle switches you to Custom. Costs are MEASURED, characterized as real EGRESS endpoints, or honestly 'unmeasured' — never invented. Profile `sets` are kept in sync with GjoaLoader's PRIVACY-PRESETS by preflight Gate N. Firefox's own settings stay in Firefox Settings; this is gjoa's surface.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.intro: Pick a profile, or tune the individual toggles below (which switches you to Custom). Profiles escalate — each is a superset of the one before it.

profiles.title: Minimal
profiles.summary: gjoa's privacy defaults — telemetry and phone-home off, Do-Not-Track on. Zero site breakage.

profiles.title: Balanced
profiles.summary: Minimal + trims cross-site referers and turns off search / URL-bar suggestions (no keystroke leak to your search engine). Negligible breakage.

profiles.title: LibreWolf-Inspired
profiles.asterisk: A curated subset of LibreWolf's privacy DEFAULTS — not LibreWolf itself: its compile-time disables and ~20 C++ patches aren't here, and this changes none of gjoa's own added surface (the adblock classifier, content actors, about: pages).
profiles.summary: Balanced + resistFingerprinting (canvas / timezone / UA spoofing + window letterboxing), strict referer policy, and extension auto-update off. Real UX cost: RFP breaks some sites.

granular.title: Resist fingerprinting (RFP)
granular.help: Spoofs canvas, timezone (UTC), and a generic UA to defeat fingerprinting. High UX cost — breaks canvas-reliant sites.

granular.title: Letterboxing
granular.help: Pads the viewport to a generic size so window dimensions can't fingerprint you. Visible grey margins.

granular.title: Cross-origin referer policy
granular.help: 0 = send full referer; 2 = send only on same-origin requests (strictest).

granular.title: Cross-origin referer trimming
granular.help: 0 = full URL; 2 = trim cross-origin referer to scheme+host+port (no path/query leak).

granular.title: Search suggestions
granular.help: Off stops sending what you type to the search engine for suggestions.

granular.title: URL-bar search suggestions
granular.help: Off stops URL-bar keystrokes reaching the search engine.

granular.title: Extension auto-update
granular.help: Off stops automatic add-on update checks (an egress + supply-chain consideration). You update manually.

granular.title: Do-Not-Track header
granular.help: Sends DNT:1. Advisory only — most sites ignore it; gjoa's native blocking is what actually stops trackers.

settings.title: Mode
settings.help: How pages are darkened. system = match the OS theme (default); hybrid = always dark (per-site native + invert the rest); auto = only sites with their own dark theme (no inversion). The legacy filter/engine modes remain available via about:config.

settings.title: Darkness
settings.help: How dark un-themed pages get. 0-100; lower = darker. The default (20) lands a comfortable dark grey (~#161616), not pure black.

settings.title: Scrim strength
settings.help: 0-255; higher = darker scrim over un-themed pages.

settings.title: Image-analysis refinement
settings.help: Optional polish; off by default (unverified refinement on top of the correctness floor).

settings.title: Allow-list (hosts)
settings.help: Comma-separated hosts to exempt from blocking (your own allow-list). Empty = block everywhere.

settings.title: List-driven scriptlets
settings.help: SECURITY: off = only curated baseline scriptlets run; on allows arbitrary +js() injection from your filter lists. Leave off unless you trust your lists.

measurement.basis: A security gate (finding F2), not a perf knob.

sections.title: Reversible features (debloat)
sections.intro: Firefox subsystems gjoa disables by default - present and reversible. gjoa never deletes a capability; it parks it behind a knob. These are EGRESS/feature toggles, not memory knobs - so the honest cost is the network endpoint each would re-contact if you toggle it on (documented Firefox behaviour), not an invented perf number. Idle-memory deltas remain unmeasured; they are not the relevant axis here.

measurement.basis: Firefox submits telemetry pings to incoming.telemetry.mozilla.org. Off in gjoa, so it never fires; toggling on re-enables that egress. Idle CPU/memory of the telemetry subsystem is unmeasured (not the relevant cost).
Firefox Health Report uploads to incoming.telemetry.mozilla.org. The cost is the data sent, not memory.

Pocket fetches recommendations/sponsored content from getpocket endpoints (surfaced on the stock new-tab, which gjoa replaces anyway). Feature toggle; no saving exists for a feature that was never running.
Disables Sync (accounts.firefox.com). Feature loss, not a measured perf win - egress only occurs if you actively sign in.
The add-ons recommendation service (TAAR) sends usage signals to services.addons.mozilla.org. Egress vector; memory cost unmeasured.
Normandy pulls remote experiment/recipe definitions from normandy.cdn.mozilla.net and can flip prefs remotely. Disabling it removes both that egress and remote mutation of your build.
Sponsored-tile placement is fetched from contile.services.mozilla.com. No functional cost (custom new-tab); the cost is the egress.

sections.title: Layout
sections.intro: How gjoa arranges tabs and chrome.

measurement.basis: Auto-switches to horizontal tabs on a narrow / half-width window and vertical (sidebar) tabs on a wide one, live as the window resizes. No egress; pure layout.

links.title: Sovereignty - egress proof
links.desc: The source-derived list of every point this build reaches the network, checked against the build you're running. A proof, not a setting.

links.title: Raw preferences
links.desc: Every gjoa.* and Firefox pref, raw. The expert escape hatch - each setting above shows its pref name to search here.
